--- Thread (50 messages, 2018-05-10) ---
[2018-05-10 00:41] itsotter: "But how do we figure out what green, blue and red are worth (we'll get to energy in part 3, it's more complicated)? Simple. Drone costs 3 gold and produces 1/3 of that cost each turn. So if we assume that the first five base set units are perfectly balanced (which they kind of automatically are since they set the standards for everything else), then a Conduit must also produce 1/3 of its value per turn, thus green is worth 1/3 the cost of a Conduit, or 1.33. By the same token, blue is worth 1.67, and red is worth exactly 1 gold."
[2018-05-10 00:43] itsotter: "production units are perfectly balanced by definition" = "they all must produce the same percent of their gold value per turn"?
[2018-05-10 00:43] itsotter: seems like many assumptions
[2018-05-10 00:45] liadahlia (Lia): which part is assumption?
[2018-05-10 00:46] f300xen (f0x): i don't get how that works, since gold lets you make more gold but rgb doesn't let you make more gold
[2018-05-10 00:46] liadahlia (Lia): rgb lets you make other things
[2018-05-10 00:46] Deleted User (Deleted User): well the base set is balanced, but it's not actually a priori knowledge
[2018-05-10 00:46] liadahlia (Lia): that generates value
[2018-05-10 00:46] liadahlia (Lia): it's not that the base set is balanced, it's that everything is balanced around the base set
[2018-05-10 00:46] liadahlia (Lia): so the base set is by definition, balanced
[2018-05-10 00:47] itsotter: that does not follow if we are being rigorous at all
[2018-05-10 00:47] wilson0862 (Wilson): Feel free to build a better model.
[2018-05-10 00:48] liadahlia (Lia): you are saying these things but you're not backing yourself up
[2018-05-10 00:48] wilson0862 (Wilson): Or suggest better assumptions with support.
[2018-05-10 00:48] wilson0862 (Wilson): Or not even support. Just better passable assumptions.
[2018-05-10 00:51] itsotter: the idea that a drone and a blastforge being "balanced" means they'll both produce gold-value in the same ratio of build cost is just an idea
[2018-05-10 00:51] Deleted User (Deleted User): basically, it's a super great approximation
[2018-05-10 00:52] itsotter: and granting this sort of arbitrary definition of balance, we don't have a demonstration that rgb -are- balanced
[2018-05-10 00:52] liadahlia (Lia): obviously you buying a blastforge with 5 gold doesn't mean the each blue is actually worth about 5/3 of a gold
[2018-05-10 00:53] liadahlia (Lia): if buying a unit with blue is worth less than buying a unit with gold, then that unit is bad and blastforge won't be bought
[2018-05-10 00:53] Deleted User (Deleted User): you don't have a demonstration. However, that is roughly true, despite not being proven
[2018-05-10 00:53] mtanzer: the high econ vivid opening for p2 is 1EE  1DD  DDDD
[2018-05-10 00:54] mtanzer: DD  DDEE comes out 1 gold worse iirc
[2018-05-10 00:54] liadahlia (Lia): why does it matter if rgb units are balanced or not
[2018-05-10 00:54] liadahlia (Lia): if a unit is weak, they won't be bought
[2018-05-10 00:55] Deleted User (Deleted User): because if it wasn't then, RGB would be worth more or less
[2018-05-10 00:55] liadahlia (Lia): obviously dev's goal is to try and make each units as balanced as they can be
[2018-05-10 00:56] mtanzer: I haven't read the entire discussion about balance but I think the main idea is to have multiple competing options available
[2018-05-10 00:56] itsotter: ah the "devs did what they set out to do perfectly" mathematical argument
[2018-05-10 00:56] mtanzer: so different units need to be close enough in strength that you don't just always prefer one over the other
[2018-05-10 00:56] Deleted User (Deleted User): I agree with your point otter that it's not obvious it's balanced
[2018-05-10 00:57] mtanzer: base set definitely isn't "automatically" balanced by being a reference point for balance or anything.  there was a time when wall had 4hp but somewhere along the line lunarch realized how imbalanced that was
[2018-05-10 01:00] liadahlia (Lia): ok, if it's justified for you to spend 6 of your gold to buy an animus instead of 2 drones, then you do it
[2018-05-10 01:00] liadahlia (Lia): if you think your animus generates more value than 2 gold per turn, then you do it
[2018-05-10 01:01] liadahlia (Lia): that's just how much producing 2 reds per turn cost, 2 gold per turn

[2018-05-11 07:36] amalloy: i don't understand in what sets amporilla is playable. it seems like in low-econ games you're never far enough ahead to build a second animus, and if you try to go high econ followed by amporilla (eg, with centurion) you're just giving your opponent time to beat you with mid-econ amporilla
[2018-05-11 07:36] amalloy: so i only ever see it bought when one player has made the mistake of playing too greedy
[2018-05-11 07:40] Deleted User (Deleted User): ie matrix, infu, xaetron, chrono, fuge, etc etc
[2018-05-11 07:40] Deleted User (Deleted User): with something like cent it becomes rps
[2018-05-11 07:41] Deleted User (Deleted User): cent enables amp but also gets countered by amp
[2018-05-11 07:42] Deleted User (Deleted User): with xaetron in particular it's v hard to pressure the amp kid
[2018-05-11 07:43] f300xen (f0x): can't forget about ebb
[2018-05-11 07:44] mtanzer: it needs support yes, so in many low econ sets it just doesn't have enough support.  but when it does have enough support (whether it's big absorber or red sink or whatever) you still don't go high econ because ampo beats high econ
[2018-05-11 07:45] mtanzer: basically you get it as soon as it's possible to do so without dying.  I usually aim for a 4 tarsier ampo and it's often on only 13 drones or maybe slightly more
[2018-05-11 07:46] Deleted User (Deleted User): seems a 4 tarsier amp is weakish
[2018-05-11 07:46] mtanzer: depends on the support.  if the support is good maybe you can get the 5th tarsier first.  I'm not saying you have to stay on 4 tarsiers after btw
[2018-05-11 07:47] mtanzer: although sometimes you're able to buy the second ampo after the first with 4 tarsiers.  if you don't have the tempo to do that you can buy another tarsier or two if you can afford it
[2018-05-11 07:56] Deleted User (Deleted User): if u don't stay on 4ts, i prefer getting the ts before the amp
[2018-05-11 07:57] Deleted User (Deleted User): i like ~5 tarsiers most and i might even prefer 6 over 4
[2018-05-11 07:59] mtanzer: if it's matrix or some other convenient big absorber I'm more likely to go for 5 or 6
[2018-05-11 08:00] mtanzer: if it's just red stuff like perf, flanimus, chrono, etc. then 5 might be too many to start with
